item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it comes from here and is "Rights Managed", making it non-compliant. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: Re: Staff Post -- Open
item.content:encoded: The choice is fine, I was just wondering. :)

item.title: Re: Staff Post -- Open
item.content:encoded:
This was one of the tales that featured the doctor that I feel gave some information to the doctor, and would give some people more information on them.

If you would like, I could change the crosslink to another tale that you think would give people more information on the character.

item.title: Re: Staff Post -- Open
item.content:encoded: Why that specific tale? :)

item.title: Staff Post -- Open
item.content:encoded:
timer removed

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show whatever
- Hide whatever

Change the following:

SCP-1119 has shown limited regenerative qualities following incident 1119-I-01. Further research warranted. - Dr. Aeslinger

To this:

SCP-1119 has shown limited regenerative qualities following incident 1119-I-01. Further research warranted. - Dr. Aeslinger

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How is a organ that is absorbed selected? It would be impossible to absorb every organ from the person he is touched by - otherwise he would be a giant blob right now to fit all those organs.

In-universe, we don't know exactly how that works. Out-of-universe, it's not that relevant to the narrative.

Also X-ray doesn't add anything to this article - from my point of view it's a normal X-ray, with some color addition? anyway no sign of superfluous organs.

Well, you have me there. I'll think about removing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How is a organ that is absorbed selected? It would be impossible to absorb every organ from the person he is touched by - otherwise he would be a giant blob right now to fit all those organs.

Also X-ray doesn't add anything to this article - from my point of view it's a normal X-ray, with some color addition? anyway no sign of superfluous organs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And this is why Google image search is not your friend. Removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvote, it's compelling, but…

http://blackcollarcrimes.com/william-bendert/

hnnnnggggg….

This makes me feel odd sensations in my stomach having a sympathetic "cool!" reaction to a picture of a convicted pedophile

Edit:

SCP-1119 - Father Goose

HNNNNNGGGGG

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Feels like his eyes should be ████████. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know how he did it, but I admire him for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did you just ask to fix a tone error with a grammar error?

I don't know how to respond to that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never mind, please delete.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
can i change just one word to make it fit the tone?

SCP-1119 is not allowed to leave his cell.

SCP-1119 is not allowed to leave its cell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was just thinking the same thing: *checks Lowest Rated Pages* Hmm, that's weird, 1119 is conspicuously absent… *checks it* Oh look, we got one that STUCK! 8D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fuck, I was hoping no one would point that out. Anyway, I'm thinking we gave him a pair we had tucked away somewhere. Probably from some poor D-Class he absorbed during testing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting how he's wearing glasses there. "Here, sir, this is our little darling, and he needs glasses. But don't touch his skin, or you'll die!"

That must be some weirded-out optometrist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can't reply anymore on this level, so @Deacon_Blues_117: look at his age and her date of passing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: well, i really like this one, specially how you handle the addendum 119-01. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll keep the offer on hand, but for now I'm fine without the answers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll PM you if you want.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How do you know he didn't? ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Speaking of the mother, why didn't he absorb her?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's terrifying to him. You know how some parents instill the fear of god in their children by telling them that strange people offering them sweets will result in [VERY BAD THINGS]? That's what this guy got from his mother, only for him it's being touched. Anywhere. Ever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ohhh, I see, he's so emotionally stunted and sheltered that he has no concept whatsoever of affection.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only if you're aware it's a void.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A void needs to be filled.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Scantron has it in one. Sure he could kink up and wear a full-body latex outfit, but this guy has no concept of physical intimacy. Never been kissed, only been touched once and that left a void.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Theoretical situation, perhaps he knew what his body would do but he really wanted a little action so he bought a nice looking hooker and covered her in a plastic tarp with a hole cut in it, perhaps she had a kink for latex body suits, perhaps he had a kink for latex body suits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How exactly do you manage to get around to fucking someone without making skin contact first? The way I see it, condoms are a moot point because he can't even hit any of the bases beforehand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good point, what would happen if there was a small tear in the condom? Is there a limit on the effects depending on the amount of exposed tissue contact?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: we need a camera SCP that outputs glamor shots, regardless of subject or setting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: condoms are wonderful things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was merely curious, I imagine having his penis stuck to a 100 lb+ object would be a pretty terrible event (especially when considering that object was someone he either deeply cared for or paid.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Judging from the picture, he probably doesn't have to worry about that too much.

Also, this in addition to your little-girl-murdering blueprints is getting you on some sort of government list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Question: it's stated that any part of him is capable of beginning absorption, during sexual interaction would his penis attach to a victim at initial contact or does it take a moment for things to bond? Would this mean that the poor bloke would have to sit there and wait for his partner to be absorbed fully before he could move?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It should be noted that his immune system isn't reacting to the absorbed tissue, like it would if they were regular organ transplants.

EDIT: Also, poor guy, the only time he interacts with anyone human, they're in a hazmat suit. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have MASTERED tone. I mean, it just sounds sciency and all in all it's a great article. Not truly terrifying unless you're him, but it's well written, which is what counts. So, I will throw it an upvote ASAP. I am posting from a phone so it may act hinky on me if I try it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 From me. I liked the implications to the fate of his family and how badly he feels about the entire ordeal. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, this is how you do an X-Man humanoid. What works best are the effects his own condition has on himself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The impossible has happened. A GOOD submission for 1119! Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, at least you're not expunging containment procedures. I've seen that happen, and it's hilarious.

Anyways, the key to a successful expungement is to use it on something that people should not be allowed to know. For example, take something infamous such as 110-Montauk. After some thought, it becomes clear to the reader exactly why an upper-level might want that data expunged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm now curious as to what exactly WAS the thought behind the expungement… You already have my upvote, but I like to know what really ticks behind authors' heads sometimes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's your call, I like the article either way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I could, but that's not what I envisioned was behind the expungement. After all, how would we find out of they were? Anyway, it was there more because of my pathological need to try and expunge something succesfully in my articles than anything else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like to imagine that whoever compiled the report wrote "They appear to be testicles." because they're just two round objects dangling there and he thought it was funny to point that out, but then a higher-level expunged it because it was immature.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: whoops delete this thought it didnt send but it did

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you could change it to "fully functional" instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Expungement/redaction is not one of my strongest points admittedly. Would removal of that sentence, since I have to agree it adds nothing, make you reconsider?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1119 has been found to have two human eyeballs attached to the interior of the medial lobe of the right lung. They appear to be [DATA EXPUNGED].

Downvoted here. There appears to be no good reason to expunge this information.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*snerk*
You win the internets.

In all seriousness, I fail to see what's the problem - the photograph serves a clear purpose of allowing whoever is to be let in to identify SCP-1119 unambiguously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would you rather the Foundation have used a colourful background for their mug-shot?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just don't rerally like it when humanoids have such generic photographs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Funny, I like the first picture. It seems to humanize him until we get to the more horrific part. A real file would have a file photo for identification, IMO.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like the first picture. Other than that though, this is pretty great. I love sad humanoids. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll think about it. I left more detailed test results out mostly because it seemed to me it would only detract from the core concept here: the fact that this man will never know what normal human contact is.

But to answer your questions: his body stores parts indefinitely, but without a detectable pattern and SCP-1119 does not retain anything, no memories, no emotions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Compelling, but it feels somewhat incomplete and unfinished.

There's too much setup before we find out what he actually does that seems to purposefully attempt to mislead. There also should be more exploration of what the cycle is for someone who is absorbed. Do they eventually vanish completely? Does he retain anything from them (memories, emotions, etc)?

Withholding a vote for now, but would very easily upvote with a bit more refining of the idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tone is great, idea is great… overall , kudos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very interesting, while kept in the realm of realism. I like it +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're not alone, Boa. And I have the complete series on DVD, so there's no excuse. And that's quite alright, MarieAlessa. Your upvote bought you more than enough credit.
